summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 7 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:101` — `staleItem.status = "ready"` mutates the `listProjectItems` result object in-place; the ready pool filter at line 106 silently depends on this. If `listProjectItems` ever returns frozen/cloned objects, stale recovery silently no-ops without error. Build the ready pool explicitly rather than relying on reference mutation. *(Carry-forward — unresolved across all prior architect evals.)*

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:173` — `process.env.PATH` is baked into the crontab line at setup time. A Node upgrade via nvm or project move silently produces a broken cron job. Add a printed note: "Re-run `agt cron-setup` after Node upgrades or project moves."

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:101` — `staleItem.status = "ready"` mutates the `items` array element in-place; the `readyItems` filter at line 106 is only correct because it runs after this loop. Moving the filter above the loop during a future refactor silently drops stale items from dispatch with no error. Document the ordering dependency with a comment, or build `readyItems` as `[...recoveredItems, ...items.filter(...)]` with a separate array.

🟡 `.team/features/cron-based-outer-loop/tasks/*/artifacts/` — No `test-output.txt` in any task artifact directory; gate output is truncated (stops mid-run, never reaches cron-tick suite); handshake claims 558 total but only 1 net-new test is visible in cron-tick.test.mjs (27 − 26 = 1), leaving claimed total of 558 (vs. 555) unverifiable by +2 — store full test runner output as a task artifact

**One persistent 🟡 carry-forward**: no test artifact stored in any task directory means the claimed 558 full-suite count cannot be independently verified from artifacts. The gate output provided is truncated and does not show cron-tick results.
Both run_1 🟡 findings are closed with direct evidence:
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` — `readProjectNumber` duplicates `outer-loop.mjs:117` body-for-body; extract to a shared helper in `util.mjs` (carry-forward from run_1; not introduced by this feature)
